title: Pipeline Bundle
description: Top-level container wrapping all records for a single pipeline run. Carries the integrity seal (bundle_hash) and the disclosure flag. Primary EU AI Act mapping: Art. 12(1), 12(2), 50(1).

bundle_id.description: Unique identifier for this bundle. Schema metadata; no direct Act mapping.

record_type.description: Discriminator identifying this object as a Pipeline Bundle.

pipeline_id.description: Identifier of the pipeline this bundle captures. EU AI Act Art. 12(2)(c) — supports operational monitoring (Art. 26(5)).

session_id.description: Identifier of the execution session within the pipeline. EU AI Act Art. 12(2)(c) — supports operational monitoring.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 timestamp marking the moment the bundle was sealed (records list frozen, bundle_hash computed). Schema metadata supporting Art. 12(2) traceability.

disclosure_presented.description: Whether an AI-interaction disclosure was presented to the user during this pipeline run. EU AI Act Art. 50(1) — users must be informed they are interacting with an AI system.

outcome.description: Terminal outcome of the pipeline run as a whole. 'completed' = the run finished normally; 'error' = the run finished but at least one step failed; 'aborted' = the run was stopped before reaching its end. EU AI Act Art. 12(2)(a) — records at the run level whether the system malfunctioned or was halted, complementing per-step status.

records.description: Ordered list of provenance records produced during the pipeline run. Each item must validate against exactly one of the Agent Step, Tool Invocation, or Human Intervention record schemas (discriminated by record_type). EU AI Act Art. 12(2) — the ordered chain is the traceability evidence.

bundle_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest computed over the canonical JSON of this bundle with the bundle_hash field itself removed. Provides a tamper-evident integrity seal for the entire record set. EU AI Act Art. 12(1) — the 'technically allow' obligation requires logs that are fit for evidentiary use, which mutable record sets are not. Verification recomputes the same exclusion-and-hash; mismatch indicates tampering. The canonical-JSON form follows RFC 8785 (JCS) — sorted keys, compact separators, UTF-8 with non-ASCII preserved, ECMAScript number formatting, no NaN/Infinity — so any verifier using a conformant JCS implementation reproduces the digest exactly.

uuid.description: RFC 4122 UUID string in lowercase hex (canonical form for the protocol).

sha256_hex.description: SHA-256 digest as 64 lowercase hex characters.
